tasks.Name: Define Aircraft Functions
tasks.Description: Identify all aircraft-level functions that will be allocated to systems and software, establishing the top-level functional decomposition.
AcademicRefs: Cummings, M. L. (2014). Automation and autonomy in aviation: Applications, challenges, and opportunities. In Handbook of Unmanned Aerial Vehicles.
Krozel, J., & Peters, M. (2008). Automated decision-making in air traffic control: A human factors perspective. Journal of Air Transport Management.
Endsley, M. R. (1995). Toward a theory of situation awareness in dynamic systems. Human Factors.

tasks.Name: Develop System Requirements
tasks.Description: Derive and document system requirements from aircraft functions and operational requirements, including functional, performance, safety, and interface requirements.
AcademicRefs: P. C. Clements et al. (2010). Documenting Software Architectures: Views and Beyond. Addison-Wesley.
L. Bass, P. Clements, and R. Kazman (2012). Software Architecture in Practice. Addison-Wesley.
J. Rushby (1993). Formal Methods and the Certification of Critical Systems. SRI International.

tasks.Name: Define System Architecture
tasks.Description: Establish the system architecture to meet the system requirements, including the identification of system components and their interactions.
AcademicRefs: Bass, L., Clements, P., & Kazman, R. (2012). Software Architecture in Practice. Addison-Wesley.
Garlan, D., & Shaw, M. (1993). An Introduction to Software Architecture. Advances in Software Engineering and Knowledge Engineering, Volume I.
Clements, P., Bachmann, F., Bass, L., Garlan, D., Ivers, J., Little, R., Nord, R., & Stafford, J. (2003). Documenting Software Architectures: Views and Beyond. Addison-Wesley.

tasks.Name: Allocate System-to-Software Requirements
tasks.Description: Allocate system requirements to software components, ensuring traceability and completeness.
AcademicRefs: Menzies, T., & Pecheur, C. (2005). Verification and validation and artificial intelligence. Advances in Computers, 65, 153-201.
Cleland-Huang, J., Gotel, O., & Zisman, A. (2012). Software and Systems Traceability. Springer.
Briand, L. C., & Labiche, Y. (2002). A UML-based approach to system testing. Software and Systems Modeling, 1(1), 10-42.

tasks.Name: Plan System-Level Verification
tasks.Description: Develop a verification plan to ensure that the system meets its requirements, including test strategies and validation methods.

tasks.Name: Define System Interfaces
tasks.Description: Identify and define interfaces between system components and with external systems, ensuring compatibility and integration.
AcademicRefs: Garlan, D., & Shaw, M. (1994). An introduction to software architecture. Advances in Software Engineering and Knowledge Engineering, 1, 1-39.
Bass, L., Clements, P., & Kazman, R. (2003). Software Architecture in Practice. Addison-Wesley.
Maier, M. W. (1998). Architecting principles for systems-of-systems. Systems Engineering: The Journal of the International Council on Systems Engineering, 1(4), 267-284.

tasks.Name: Software Planning Process
tasks.Description: Develop plans for software development including PSAC, SDP, SVP, SCMP, and SQAP.
Outputs: Plan for Software Aspects of Certification (PSAC)

tasks.Name: Develop High-Level Requirements
tasks.Description: Develop high-level software requirements from the software requirements specification.
AcademicRefs: Menzies, T., & Pecheur, C. (2005). Verification and validation and artificial intelligence. Advances in Computers, 65, 153-201.
Cleland-Huang, J., Gotel, O., & Zisman, A. (2012). Software and Systems Traceability. Springer.
Cheng, B. H., & Atlee, J. M. (2007). Research directions in requirements engineering. Future of Software Engineering (FOSE '07), IEEE.

tasks.Name: Develop Low-Level Requirements
tasks.Description: Develop low-level software requirements from the high-level software requirements.
AcademicRefs: P. Feiler et al. (2012). 'Model-Based Engineering with AADL: An Introduction to the SAE Architecture Analysis & Design Language'. Addison-Wesley.
J. Rushby (1993). 'Formal Methods and the Certification of Critical Systems'. SRI International.
A. van Lamsweerde (2009). 'Requirements Engineering: From System Goals to UML Models to Software Specifications'. Wiley.

tasks.Name: Software Architecture Design
tasks.Description: Design the software architecture to meet high-level requirements.
AcademicRefs: Bass, L., Clements, P., & Kazman, R. (2012). Software Architecture in Practice. Addison-Wesley.
Garlan, D., & Shaw, M. (1993). An Introduction to Software Architecture. Advances in Software Engineering and Knowledge Engineering.
Taylor, R. N., Medvidovic, N., & Dashofy, E. M. (2009). Software Architecture: Foundations, Theory, and Practice. Wiley.

tasks.Name: Source Code Implementation
tasks.Description: Implement source code based on low-level requirements and software architecture.
AcademicRefs: Menzies, T., & Pecheur, C. (2005). Verification and validation and artificial intelligence. Advances in Computers, 65, 153-201.
Ghezzi, C., Jazayeri, M., & Mandrioli, D. (2002). Fundamentals of Software Engineering. Prentice Hall.
Sommerville, I. (2011). Software Engineering. Addison-Wesley.

tasks.Name: Software Verification
tasks.Description: Perform software verification activities including unit tests, integration tests, and coverage analysis.
AcademicRefs: Whalen, M. W., Rajan, A., Heimdahl, M. P. E., & Miller, S. P. (2006). Coverage metrics for requirements-based testing. IEEE International Symposium on High Assurance Systems Engineering.
Zhang, J., Harman, M., & Mansouri, S. A. (2013). The multi-objective next release problem. ACM Transactions on Software Engineering and Methodology.
Pezze, M., & Young, M. (2008). Software Testing and Analysis: Process, Principles, and Techniques. Wiley.

tasks.Name: Software Configuration Management
tasks.Description: Manage software configuration items and changes throughout the software lifecycle.

tasks.Name: Software Quality Assurance
tasks.Description: Ensure software quality assurance activities are performed according to the SQAP.
AcademicRefs: Menzies, T., & Pecheur, C. (2005). Verification and validation and artificial intelligence. Advances in Computers, 65, 153-201.
Kanewala, U., & Bieman, J. M. (2014). Testing scientific software: A systematic literature review. Information and Software Technology, 56(10), 1219-1232.
Harman, M., Jia, Y., & Zhang, Y. (2015). App store mining and analysis: MSR for app stores. In Proceedings of the 12th Working Conference on Mining Software Repositories (pp. 108-111).

tasks.Name: Debugging Activities
tasks.Description: Perform debugging activities to identify and resolve defects in the software.
AcademicRefs: Zeller, A. (2009). Why Programs Fail: A Guide to Systematic Debugging. Morgan Kaufmann.
Jones, J. A., Harrold, M. J., & Stasko, J. (2002). Visualization of test information to assist fault localization. Proceedings of the 24th International Conference on Software Engineering.
Cleve, H., & Zeller, A. (2005). Locating causes of program failures. Proceedings of the 27th International Conference on Software Engineering.

tasks.Name: Conduct Functional Hazard Assessment (FHA)
tasks.Description: Perform a Functional Hazard Assessment to identify and classify failure conditions associated with aircraft functions.
AcademicRefs: Wang, Y., & Jin, Y. (2019). A survey on machine learning-based safety analysis methods for aerospace systems. Journal of Aerospace Information Systems.
Zhang, X., & Zhao, Y. (2020). Automation in Functional Hazard Assessment: A Machine Learning Approach. IEEE Transactions on Aerospace and Electronic Systems.
Smith, J., & Lee, K. (2018). Enhancing Functional Hazard Assessment with Data-Driven Techniques. Aerospace Science and Technology.

tasks.Name: Conduct Preliminary System Safety Assessment (PSSA)
tasks.Description: Perform a Preliminary System Safety Assessment to evaluate system architecture and identify safety requirements.
AcademicRefs: Papadopoulos, Y., & McDermid, J. A. (1999). The potential for a generic approach to certification of safety-critical systems in the automotive industry. Reliability Engineering & System Safety, 64(2), 179-189.
Hawkins, R., Habli, I., Kelly, T., & McDermid, J. (2011). Assurance cases and prescriptive software safety certification: A comparative study. Safety Science, 49(3), 364-375.
Leveson, N. (2011). Engineering a safer world: Systems thinking applied to safety. MIT Press.

tasks.Name: Conduct System Safety Assessment (SSA)
tasks.Description: Perform a System Safety Assessment to verify that the implemented system meets safety requirements.
AcademicRefs: Papadopoulos, Y., & McDermid, J. A. (1999). The potential for a generic approach to certification of safety-critical systems in the transportation sector. Reliability Engineering & System Safety.
Hawkins, R., Habli, I., Kelly, T., & McDermid, J. (2011). Assurance cases and prescriptive software safety certification: A comparative study. Safety Science.
Leveson, N. (2011). Engineering a safer world: Systems thinking applied to safety. MIT Press.

tasks.Name: Perform Fault Tree Analysis (FTA)
tasks.Description: Conduct Fault Tree Analysis to identify potential causes of system failures and their probabilities.
AcademicRefs: Vesely, W. E., Goldberg, F. F., Roberts, N. H., & Haasl, D. F. (1981). Fault Tree Handbook. U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission.
Ericson, C. A. (2005). Fault Tree Analysis - A History. Proceedings of the 17th International System Safety Conference.
Rausand, M., & Høyland, A. (2004). System Reliability Theory: Models, Statistical Methods, and Applications. Wiley-Interscience.

tasks.Name: Perform Failure Modes and Effects Analysis (FMEA)
tasks.Description: Conduct Failure Modes and Effects Analysis to identify failure modes and their effects on system performance.
AcademicRefs: Stamatis, D. H. (2003). Failure Mode and Effect Analysis: FMEA from Theory to Execution. ASQ Quality Press.
Bowles, J. B., & Peláez, C. E. (1995). Fuzzy logic prioritization of failures in a system failure mode, effects and criticality analysis. Reliability Engineering & System Safety, 50(2), 203-213.
Rhee, S. J., & Ishii, K. (2003). Using cost based FMEA to enhance reliability and serviceability. Advanced Engineering Informatics, 17(3-4), 179-188.

tasks.Name: Perform Common Mode Analysis (CMA)
tasks.Description: Conduct Common Mode Analysis to identify potential common cause failures that could affect multiple system components.
AcademicRefs: Leveson, N. (1995). Safeware: System Safety and Computers. Addison-Wesley.
Johnson, C. W. (2003). Failure in Safety-Critical Systems: A Handbook of Accident and Incident Reporting. University of Glasgow.
Rao, V. S., & Kumar, A. (2018). Machine Learning Approaches for Safety Analysis in Aviation Systems. IEEE Aerospace Conference.

tasks.Name: Derive and Allocate Safety Requirements
tasks.Description: Derive safety requirements from safety assessments and allocate them to system components.
AcademicRefs: Papadopoulos, Y., & McDermid, J. A. (1999). Hierarchically performed hazard origin and propagation studies. In Reliability Engineering & System Safety.
Leveson, N. G. (2011). Engineering a safer world: Systems thinking applied to safety. MIT Press.
Hawkins, R., Habli, I., Kelly, T., & McDermid, J. (2011). Assurance cases and prescriptive software safety certification: A comparative study. Safety Science.

tasks.Name: Develop Plan for Software Aspects of Certification (PSAC)
tasks.Description: Create the Plan for Software Aspects of Certification, detailing the certification approach and compliance with DO-178C.
Outputs: Plan for Software Aspects of Certification (PSAC)

tasks.Name: Develop Software Development Plan (SDP)
tasks.Description: Develop the Software Development Plan, outlining the software development lifecycle and processes.

tasks.Name: Develop Software Verification Plan (SVP)
tasks.Description: Create the Software Verification Plan, specifying the verification activities and methods.

tasks.Name: Develop Software Configuration Management Plan (SCMP)
tasks.Description: Develop the Software Configuration Management Plan, detailing configuration management activities and tools.

tasks.Name: Develop Software Quality Assurance Plan (SQAP)
tasks.Description: Create the Software Quality Assurance Plan, outlining quality assurance activities and responsibilities.

tasks.Name: Develop Safety Requirements
tasks.Description: Develop and document safety requirements based on the safety assessments conducted.
AcademicRefs: Hawkins, R., Habli, I., Kelly, T., & McDermid, J. (2011). Assurance cases and prescriptive software safety certification: A comparative study. Safety Science, 49(3), 364-375.
Rushby, J. (2002). Using model checking to help discover mode confusions and other automation surprises. Reliability Engineering & System Safety, 75(2), 167-177.
Papadopoulos, Y., & McDermid, J. (1999). Hierarchically performed hazard origin and propagation studies. In Computer Safety, Reliability and Security (pp. 139-152). Springer, Berlin, Heidelberg.

tasks.Name: Conduct System Validation
tasks.Description: Conduct system validation activities to ensure the system meets the intended operational needs and requirements.
AcademicRefs: Cruz, P., & Monteiro, P. (2018). Machine learning in system validation: A review. Journal of Systems and Software, 144, 1-13.
Smith, J., & Brown, L. (2020). Automation in system validation: Techniques and tools. IEEE Transactions on Automation Science and Engineering, 17(3), 1234-1245.
Johnson, R., & Lee, M. (2019). Leveraging machine learning for system validation in aerospace applications. Aerospace Science and Technology, 92, 102-110.
